name: Hyrox Peak & Taper — Simulation Rescheduled
goal: Event readiness for Hyrox on April 29. Move simulation from today to Wednesday April 22 — DOMS, suppressed HRV, and ACWR 1.4 all indicate today is not the day. Let acute load settle, execute the simulation mid-week in the optimal ACWR window, then run a clean 6-day taper to arrive race-ready. Maintain Fitbod strength scores above 58.
notes:
NO STRUCTURAL CHANGES FROM EXISTING PLAN: The ai-plan-2026-04-19 structure is carried forward as-is. Today's data (HRV 69ms — 26% below average, DOMS still present from Friday's 75kg deadlift PR, ACWR 1.4) confirms the decision to rest today and delay the simulation to Wednesday. There is no reason to deviate from the current plan — it was built for exactly this scenario and the data continues to support it.

SIMULATION MOVED TO WEDNESDAY APRIL 22: Three independent signals confirm this is the right call. (1) HRV at 69ms is 26% below your 7-day average of 93ms — your autonomic nervous system is signalling incomplete recovery. (2) DOMS from Friday's deadlift session (75kg PR) means your posterior chain and legs are not ready for 8km of running plus sled work and lunges. (3) ACWR is 1.4, above the 1.3 injury-risk threshold — a full simulation today would spike acute load further into the danger zone. Moving to Wednesday gives 3 more days for DOMS to fully resolve, HRV to recover, and the heavier sessions from April 11–17 to age out of the 7-day acute window.

7 DAYS OUT vs 10 DAYS OUT: The original plan placed the simulation 10 days pre-race. At 7 days out you're at the shorter end of the acceptable window, but this is still fine — especially since the simulation is at 70% effort, not maximal. Many athletes do their final key session 7 days out with excellent results. The 6-day taper afterwards is clean.

ACWR PROJECTION: By Wednesday April 22, the 7-day acute window (April 16–22) will contain only the April 17 leg session (~moderate TRIMP) and April 20 upper body pull (~low TRIMP). The heavier April 11 simulation and April 15 zone 2 run will have aged out. Estimated ACWR by simulation day: ~1.0–1.1, squarely in the optimal window.

SHOULDER INJURY — NON-NEGOTIABLE CONSTRAINT: Right rotator cuff tear (moderate, active since April 14). BANNED movements in ALL sessions: overhead press, lateral raises, upright rows, behind-neck press, dips, wide-grip overhead pulling. Two Hyrox stations are affected: (1) Ski Erg — use shortened ROM at top, drive power from lats and hips, conservative pace. (2) Wall Balls — the critical shoulder test. Wednesday's simulation MUST assess wall ball tolerance: start with 10 reps and rate pain 0–10. This data determines race-day wall ball strategy. Burpee broad jumps — if push-up component aggravates the shoulder, modify to squat-thrust broad jumps. All other stations (sled push/pull, rowing, farmer's carry, sandbag lunges) are safe at neutral angles.

RACE DAY PACING: Use April 22 simulation data to set race-day targets. Run legs at 5:30–6:00/km (deliberately slower than your 5:16 interval pace). Negative-split if possible: start at 6:00/km, work down to 5:30 in the final 2–3 legs. Station order: Ski Erg → Sled Push → Sled Pull → Burpee Broad Jumps → Rowing → Farmer's Carry → Sandbag Lunges → Wall Balls.

RACE DAY NUTRITION: Eat a familiar carb-rich meal 2–3 hours before start. Banana or gel 30 min before. Sip water — don't overhydrate. Gel or fast carb source after station 4–5 if energy drops. Pack a post-race recovery meal.

CARB LOADING: Begin April 26 (3 days out). Increase carbohydrates to 6–8g/kg (~425–565g). Familiar sources: rice, pasta, bread, potatoes, fruit, oats. Maintain protein at ~1.6g/kg (~113g).

FITBOD SCORES: Push 62, Pull 60, Legs 60, Overall 61 — all above the 58 floor. One upper body pull session on April 20 maintains the pull score. Legs at 60 from Friday's session will hold through the taper. Push at 62 has headroom and no push session is programmed — the shoulder injury makes push movements too risky. All scores will comfortably hold above 58.

WEIGHT TREND: Maintain caloric surplus through the simulation, then eat to maintenance during taper. Prioritise carb-loading the final 3 days (April 26–28).

phases.name: Phase 1 — Recovery & Race Simulation
phases.focus: Let ACWR drop from 1.4 back into the 1.0–1.2 range before executing the key simulation session. Recover from Friday's DOMS and last night's suppressed HRV (69ms vs 93ms average). One light upper body pull session on Monday maintains Fitbod scores while respecting the shoulder injury. Full rest Tuesday to arrive fresh for the simulation. By Wednesday, legs will be fully recovered (5 days post-deadlift), HRV should be trending back to baseline, and ACWR will be in the optimal window (~1.0–1.1).

days.session: Full rest day. Do not train. You have three clear reasons to rest today: (1) DOMS from Friday's deadlift session (75kg PR — well done) still present in posterior chain, (2) HRV at 69ms is 26% below your 7-day average of 93ms — your autonomic nervous system hasn't recovered, (3) ACWR is 1.4 and needs to drop before the simulation. Walk up to 30 min if desired but no structured exercise. Foam roll quads, hamstrings, glutes, and calves if sore. Eat well — prioritise protein (aim 113g+ across the day, ~1.6g/kg) and carbohydrates for glycogen replenishment. Hydrate consistently. Aim for 8+ hours sleep tonight — this is the single most important recovery action today. Last night's 9.12h of sleep was good duration but quality was poor (HRV 69ms); tonight focus on sleep hygiene: dark room, no screens 30 min before bed, cool temperature. The simulation is now Wednesday — you have 3 full days to arrive completely fresh.

Upper body pull focus — ~35–40 min at RPE 6. This is a maintenance session to hold your pull score at ≥60 heading into the taper, not a builder.

⚠ SHOULDER INJURY PROTOCOL — RIGHT ROTATOR CUFF TEAR: The following movements are BANNED: overhead press, lateral raises, upright rows, behind-neck press, dips, wide-grip lat pulldown. Do NOT attempt any movement that loads the shoulder in internal rotation or full overhead flexion.

WARM-UP (5 min — rotator cuff rehab/prehab): 2 × 15 band external rotations per arm (slow, controlled), 2 × 15 band pull-aparts (squeeze shoulder blades at end range), 2 × 12 light cable or band face pulls. If any of these cause sharp pain in the right shoulder, stop that movement and note it. These double as rehab — do them deliberately.

MAIN SESSION (Fitbod — pull focus): Barbell or dumbbell rows (safe — horizontal pull at neutral angle), neutral-grip lat pulldown (palms facing each other, shoulder-width — safest grip for your shoulder; do NOT use wide overhand grip), cable seated rows (neutral or underhand grip), face pulls (heavier than warm-up set — 3 working sets), bicep curls (safe — no shoulder load). 3 working sets per exercise. RPE 6 on all working sets — stop 3–4 reps short of failure. Do not push to failure on any set.

PULL-UP MODIFICATION: Chin-ups (palms facing you) or neutral-grip pull-ups are generally tolerable for rotator cuff injuries. Attempt 2 sets at moderate effort — if any right shoulder pain, swap immediately to neutral-grip lat pulldowns. Do NOT attempt wide-grip pull-ups.

COOL DOWN: Repeat the band external rotations and pull-aparts from warm-up — 1 set each. Gentle shoulder ROM circles.

Log Fitbod scores post-session. This is the LAST strength session before Hyrox — everything from here is taper and activation only.

Full rest. This is your pre-simulation recovery day. Walk, stretch gently, foam roll lightly if needed. No structured exercise.

PREPARATION FOR TOMORROW:
• Eat a larger meal with extra carbohydrates at dinner — you're fuelling for tomorrow's simulation. Familiar foods only — nothing new.
• Lay out your kit and confirm equipment availability at your gym: ski erg, rower, sled, wall balls, farmer's carry handles, sandbag.
• Review the station order and your pacing plan: 5:30–6:00/km run legs, conservative starts on ski erg (2:10–2:20/500m with shortened ROM) and rower (2:00–2:10/500m), sustainable rhythm on burpee broad jumps, wall balls in sets of 10–15 starting conservatively to test the shoulder.
• Mentally walk through each station transition — jog in, set up, find rhythm, finish, move to next run leg.
• Aim for 8+ hours sleep — make this non-negotiable. Legs should feel completely fresh (5 days post-deadlift, DOMS fully resolved), and HRV should be trending back toward your 93ms average.
• Hydrate well throughout the day.

Full Hyrox race format at controlled 70% effort. This is the most important session in the entire program — a full dress rehearsal 7 days before race day. By today ACWR should be ~1.0–1.1 (optimal). Structure: 8 × (1km run + station) in official order.

⚠ SHOULDER INJURY — RIGHT ROTATOR CUFF TEAR: Affects Ski Erg and Wall Balls most directly. Read station-specific notes carefully. If sharp pain (>6/10) occurs at any station, STOP that station, note the issue, and move on. The goal is data collection and confidence-building, not heroics.

PRE-SESSION: Warm up 15 min — 5 min easy jog, dynamic stretches (leg swings, hip circles, arm circles — small range only on right shoulder), 5 easy wall balls to test shoulder, 250m easy row. Band external rotations and pull-aparts for rotator cuff (2 × 10 each).

RUN LEGS: Target 5:30–6:00/km — deliberately slower than your 5:16 interval pace from April 11. You're doing 8 of them with stations between. Walk 2–3 min between run+station if needed. Aim for CONSISTENT splits rather than fast splits — the goal is to learn your sustainable race pace.

STATION ORDER AND CUES:
(1) SKI ERG 1000m — ⚠ SHOULDER MODIFICATION: Use a shortened range of motion at the top — do NOT reach fully overhead. Drive power from hip hinge and lat engagement, not shoulder flexion. Conservative pace: 2:10–2:20/500m split. If sharp right shoulder pain at any point, stop and substitute with assault bike 50 cal. Rate shoulder pain 0–10 after.
(2) SLED PUSH 50m — Drive from hips, short powerful steps, arms at chest height (neutral shoulder position — safe). Steady and controlled.
(3) SLED PULL 50m — Hand-over-hand, sit back and use bodyweight. Horizontal pulling at neutral angle — generally tolerable for rotator cuff. Controlled rhythm.
(4) BURPEE BROAD JUMPS 80m — Sustainable rhythm. HR spikes hardest here. If the push-up component causes right shoulder pain, modify to squat-thrust broad jumps (skip the push-up — drop to squat, jump forward). Note if modification was needed.
(5) ROWING 1000m — Horizontal pull at low angle (safe for shoulder). Hold a steady 2:00–2:10/500m split. Don't sprint the first 500m.
(6) FARMER'S CARRY 200m — Arms at sides, grip and posture. Fully shoulder-safe. Squeeze handles hard, shoulders back and down. Don't rush.
(7) SANDBAG LUNGES 100m — Bear-hug the sandbag at chest height. If the grip position bothers the right shoulder, shift more weight to the left side. Core tight, controlled steps, conserve energy for wall balls. Don't overstride.
(8) WALL BALLS 100 reps — ⚠ CRITICAL SHOULDER TEST: This is the most shoulder-demanding station (repetitive squat-to-overhead throw). Start with a set of 10 and assess pain. If tolerable (pain ≤3/10): continue in sets of 15–20 with 5–10 sec rest. If moderate pain (4–6/10): reduce to sets of 8–10 with longer rest, accept a slower time. If sharp pain (>6/10): STOP wall balls, note the rep count reached, substitute remaining reps with goblet squats. This data is ESSENTIAL — you must know your wall ball tolerance before April 29.

LOG EVERYTHING: Total elapsed time, each 1km run split, time per station, RPE per station (1–10), shoulder pain per station (0–10), overall RPE, avg and max HR. This data becomes your race-day pacing plan.

SUBSTITUTIONS IF EQUIPMENT UNAVAILABLE: Ski erg → assault bike 50 cal, sled push/pull → heavy prowler or loaded farmer's carry 100m, sandbag lunges → walking lunges with dumbbells (20–24kg each).

COOL DOWN: 5 min walk. Stretch hamstrings, quads, hip flexors, calves. Gentle shoulder ROM — no aggressive stretching. Band external rotations (1 × 15 each arm). Eat within 60 min — high carb + protein (aim 40g+ protein, 80g+ carbs). Note which stations felt hardest and which muscle groups were unexpectedly fatigued — this directly informs race-day strategy.

phases.name: Phase 2 — Taper
phases.focus: Arrive at Hyrox fresh, confident, and fully fuelled. Volume cut 30–40% from normal. No new training stimulus. Brief touches of race-specific movement to stay sharp without creating fatigue. Priorities: sleep 8+ hours every night, nutrition (carb-load final 3 days starting April 26), hydration (clear urine throughout the day), and mental preparation. No strength training — Fitbod scores will hold for 7+ days without stimulus. The simulation data from April 22 is your race-day playbook — study it.

days.session:
Full rest. Do not train. Walk up to 20 min if desired. The simulation is the highest-load session in this plan and your body needs to absorb it. Foam roll any sore areas — expect quads, glutes, grip, and possibly the right shoulder to be the main fatigue sites. If the shoulder feels inflamed, apply ice for 15 min and avoid any overhead reaching for the rest of the day.

Eat at a surplus — prioritise protein (1.6g/kg = ~113g) and carbohydrates for glycogen replenishment. Aim for 8+ hours sleep.

REVIEW YOUR SIMULATION DATA: Write down total time, each run split, each station time, station RPEs, and shoulder pain ratings. Identify your fastest and slowest run legs, easiest and hardest stations. Specifically note: (1) Did your run pace hold consistent or did you slow in later legs? (2) Which stations spiked your HR the most? (3) How did the shoulder handle ski erg and wall balls? (4) Where did you feel strongest? This becomes your race-day pacing plan and strategy.

20 min at STRICT 120–130 bpm. Heart rate controlled — not pace controlled. Based on your training data, expect ~9:00–9:30/km or slower on the treadmill. If HR drifts above 130, walk until it drops below 125, then resume. Do not chase pace under any circumstances.

Legs should feel light after a full rest day post-simulation. If anything feels off — unusual soreness, heavy legs, lingering fatigue — cut to 15 min or replace entirely with a 20 min walk. No shame in walking — the fitness is banked.

Purpose: blood flow to aid recovery, maintain running rhythm, and confirm your legs are bouncing back well from the simulation. This is the last run over 10 min before race day. Log average HR.

days.focus: Light Hyrox Station Walk-Through — Mental Rehearsal
days.session:
20–25 min at 50% effort maximum. This is mental rehearsal and movement pattern priming, not physical stimulus.

⚠ SHOULDER: Only touch ski erg and wall balls if they were pain-free (≤2/10) during Wednesday's simulation. If they caused any meaningful pain, skip them entirely today.

Touch each pattern briefly: Ski erg 250m easy (shortened ROM, lat-driven — skip if shoulder was sore Wednesday), Rower 250m easy, 5 wall balls at easy pace (skip if shoulder was painful Wednesday — substitute 10 goblet squats), 10 goblet squats, 25m farmer's carry, 10 bodyweight lunges, 5 burpees at half speed.

Between each movement: walk and visualise the race-day transition — jog into the station, set up quickly, find your rhythm, finish and move to the next run leg. Mentally rehearse: your pre-race meal and timing, your pacing targets from the simulation data, your wall ball set/rest strategy, your warm-up routine.

You should finish feeling confident and mentally sharp, not physically taxed. If anything feels heavy or sluggish, stop immediately — the fitness is banked and the taper is working.

Full rest. No training. Light walk OK (15–20 min max). Begin carb-loading today — increase carbohydrate intake to 6–8g/kg bodyweight (~425–565g). Focus on familiar, easy-to-digest sources: rice, pasta, bread, potatoes, fruit, oats, honey. Maintain protein at ~1.6g/kg (~113g). Hydrate consistently — aim for clear urine.

You may feel bloated from the increased carbs — this is normal. Each gram of glycogen stores ~3g of water, so expect 1–2kg of water weight gain. This is fuel, not fat.

Sleep 8+ hours. Foam roll gently if any residual tightness. Lay out or confirm race-day kit: shoes (broken in, not new), socks, shorts, shirt, watch (fully charged), grip aids for farmer's carry if using (chalk, gloves). Confirm event logistics — travel time, parking, check-in time, wave start time.

15 min total — very light, just enough to prime movement patterns and keep the nervous system engaged. You should feel snappy and energised afterwards, not fatigued.

Structure: 5 min easy walk to warm up, then: 10 goblet squats (light weight), 5 wall balls at easy pace (ONLY if shoulder was pain-free during Wednesday's simulation and Saturday's walk-through — otherwise substitute 10 additional goblet squats), 200m ski erg easy with shortened ROM (skip if shoulder sore — substitute 1 min easy assault bike or skip entirely), 200m row easy, 5 burpees at half speed. Cool down with 5 min walk.

If anything feels heavy, sluggish, or painful, stop immediately. The fitness is banked. This is a nervous system check-in, not a training session.

Continue carb-loading (same targets as yesterday: 6–8g/kg carbs, 1.6g/kg protein). Sleep 8+ hours tonight — this is the most important night of sleep in the entire plan.

Review your simulation splits one final time and commit to your race-day pacing targets. Write them on your hand or phone if needed.

Full rest. Final preparation day. OPTIONAL: 10 min very light jog at pure conversational pace to settle pre-race nerves — only if this is something that helps you mentally. If you feel good and rested, skip it entirely. Do NOT do any intensity. No stretching to new ranges of motion.

NUTRITION: Continue carb-loading. Eat a familiar carb-rich dinner — ideally the same type of meal you'll eat pre-race tomorrow. If your event is morning, rehearse your pre-race meal timing today (eat the same meal at the same time gap before bed as you'll use before the race). Hydrate well but taper fluid intake 2 hours before bed to avoid disrupted sleep.

RACE DAY PLAN — FINAL REVIEW:
• Pre-race meal: 2–3 hours before start. Familiar, carb-rich, easy to digest (e.g. rice + chicken, pasta + sauce, oatmeal + banana). Banana or gel 30 min before start. Sip water — don't overhydrate.
• Warm-up: 15 min easy jog, dynamic stretches, band external rotations for shoulder, 5 practice wall balls (if shoulder allows), 250m easy row.
• Run pacing: Start at 6:00/km for the first 2–3 legs. Build to 5:30/km in the later legs if you feel good. Consistent > fast.
• Station strategy: Steady rhythm on everything. Don't rush transitions. Ski erg — shortened ROM, lat-driven, conservative 2:10–2:20/500m. Wall balls — use the set/rest strategy you tested Wednesday. Save something for the final 2 stations.
• Shoulder management: If pain spikes above 6/10 on any station, modify or slow down. Don't push through sharp pain.
• Mid-race fuel: Gel or fast carb after station 4–5 if energy drops.
• Mantra: Start conservative, build through the middle, give everything on the last two stations.
• Post-race: Protein shake + banana or sandwich within 30 min. You've earned it.

Bed as early as possible. Tomorrow is race day — the work is done. Trust the process.
